task_description: Create a new spreadsheet, merge and center text across the top row, and save the file to the desktop with the specified name.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a desktop shortcut with the green Microsoft Excel icon—a white spreadsheet grid with a bold white 'X' on a dark green panel—labeled 'Excel 2016'. A semitransparent red X is centered directly on this shortcut, and the icon is highlighted as selected. In the full-screen view, this is on the Windows desktop near the upper-left area among other shortcuts such as 'PowerPoint 2016', '此电脑', and '网络'.
caption.think: The user intends to open the Excel 2016 desktop shortcut. Because the raw action is a double click and the target is a desktop application icon, the expected result is launching the program.
caption.expectation: Microsoft Excel 2016 should start opening, and an Excel window or splash screen should appear.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the top-right corner of the Excel window, with a semitransparent red X centered on the middle title-bar button marked by a single underscore-like horizontal line. This is the Minimize button, a toolbar/window control in the top area, positioned between the Help/question-mark area and the Maximize/Restore and Close buttons. In the full-screen view, Excel 2016 is open on its start page over the Windows desktop, showing recent templates and Chinese interface text.
caption.think: The user likely wants to minimize the Excel window to get it out of the way and return to the desktop while keeping Excel running.
caption.action: Click the Minimize button in the Excel window's top-right title bar.
caption.expectation: The Excel window should minimize to the taskbar, revealing the Windows desktop behind it.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the first workbook template thumbnail: a miniature Excel grid with column letters A, B, and C and the top-left cell outlined in green. The semitransparent red X is placed over the middle of this thumbnail, indicating the target is the template tile itself, which is a UI control rather than a worksheet cell. In the full-screen view, Excel 2016 is on its start page in Chinese, and this tile is the leftmost template option labeled '空白工作簿' (Blank workbook).
caption.think: The user likely wants to create a new blank Excel workbook from the start screen.
caption.action: Click the '空白工作簿' (Blank workbook) template tile.
caption.expectation: A new blank Excel workbook should open, leaving the start page and showing an editable worksheet grid.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the active worksheet cell at the top-left of the grid, cell A1, outlined in green with a small fill handle square. The text entry area now contains the letters "AA", visible inside cell A1, and the formula bar also shows "AA", indicating text is being entered into the currently selected cell rather than a separate dialog or search box. In the full-screen view, Microsoft Excel is open with a blank workbook on Sheet1, Chinese ribbon tabs across the top, and the insertion focus remains in the first cell of the worksheet.
caption.think: The user is entering text into the first spreadsheet cell to populate the blank workbook. Since the action is a Type command for "AAA", the intended effect is to place that text into cell A1, likely replacing the empty cell content as it is typed.
caption.action: Type "AAA" into the currently active worksheet cell A1.
caption.expectation: Cell A1 should display "AAA" and the formula bar should match, with the text entry committed or still in edit mode depending on whether Enter is pressed next.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on cell B1, the empty worksheet cell immediately to the right of A1. Cell A1 contains the text "AAA" and is currently outlined with a green selection border, while B1 under the X is a blank grid cell on the canvas rather than a toolbar control. In the full-screen view, Microsoft Excel displays a blank worksheet on Sheet1 with the formula bar showing "AAA", indicating the user is working in the grid near the top-left of the sheet.
caption.think: After typing "AAA" into A1, the user likely wants to move the active selection to the adjacent cell B1 so they can continue entering data in the next column.
caption.action: Click the blank worksheet cell B1.
caption.expectation: The selection should move from A1 to B1, making B1 the active cell with a green border, while A1 remains filled with "AAA".

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the green-outlined active cell at the top-left of the worksheet containing the text "AAA". A semitransparent red drag path begins from that selected cell and runs horizontally to the right across the first row, indicating a left-to-right drag starting from the filled cell. In the full-screen view, Microsoft Excel displays a blank worksheet on Sheet1, with "AAA" visible in cell A1 and the rest of row 1 cells to the right still empty, suggesting a range-fill or copy drag across adjacent cells.
caption.think: The user likely wants to extend the value from A1 across the first row by dragging from the selected cell toward neighboring cells on the right.
caption.action: Click-and-hold on the selected cell containing "AAA" at the start of row 1, drag along the shown path horizontally to the right across the adjacent cells in the same row, then release.
caption.expectation: The dragged range across row 1 should become selected or filled from the starting cell through the destination area, likely propagating "AAA" across the dragged cells.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the Ribbon alignment control labeled "合并居中," which is the Merge & Center button with a small dropdown arrow beside it. Nearby are other alignment controls such as horizontal alignment icons and the "自动换行" Wrap Text option, confirming this is a toolbar control in the top Ribbon area rather than a worksheet cell. In the full-screen view, Excel is open on a worksheet where cell A1 contains "AAA" and the selection appears extended across the first row from A1 toward the right, so the click is aimed at the Ribbon command above the sheet.
caption.think: The user likely wants to merge the currently selected cells across the top row and center the text "AAA" within the merged area.
caption.action: Click the "合并居中" (Merge & Center) button on the Ribbon.
caption.expectation: The selected cells should be merged into one wider cell and the text should become centered within it; the worksheet selection will update to the merged cell.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the green tab labeled "文件" at the top-left of the Excel window. This is a Ribbon/tab control in the top area, next to the currently active "开始" tab, not a worksheet cell or canvas object. In the full-screen view, Excel is open with a merged and centered cell across the top row containing "AAA"; the Ribbon is visible and the click is aimed at switching from the Home tab to the File tab.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open Excel's File backstage view to access file-level commands such as save, open, or options.
caption.action: Click the "文件" tab on the Ribbon.
caption.expectation: Excel should switch from the worksheet view to the File backstage menu, opening file-related options and replacing the current Ribbon content.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the green left-sidebar menu item labeled "另存为". It is a UI control in Excel's File backstage navigation panel, positioned between "保存" and "另存为 Adobe PDF". In the full-screen view, Excel is already in the File backstage area on the "信息" page, with file-related options displayed in the main pane, so the click is aimed at switching to the Save As section.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the Save As page to choose a location, file name, or file format for saving the workbook.
caption.action: Click the "另存为" option in the left File menu.
caption.expectation: Excel should switch from the current Information page to the Save As page, showing save-location and file-saving options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the mint-green tile labeled "这台电脑" with a small monitor/computer icon to its left. This is a UI control in Excel's Save As page, not a worksheet object or blank area. In the full-screen view, Excel is in the 文件 > 另存为 backstage screen, with location choices including OneDrive, "这台电脑", "添加位置", and "浏览"; the "这台电脑" option is already highlighted and the right pane shows common folders such as "文档" and "桌面".
caption.think: The user likely wants to choose saving the workbook to this PC rather than to OneDrive, so Excel can show local save destinations or proceed toward a local file-save dialog.
caption.action: Click the "这台电脑" tile in the Save As locations list.
caption.expectation: Excel should keep or confirm "这台电脑" as the selected save location and update the Save As area to local PC destinations, preparing for selection of a folder or opening local save options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the row labeled "桌面" with a yellow folder icon to its left. This is a selectable folder/location entry in Excel's Save As page, not a blank area or worksheet object. In the full-screen view, Excel is in 文件 > 另存为 with "这台电脑" already selected in the left column, and the main pane lists local destinations including "文档" and the highlighted target "桌面".
caption.think: The user likely wants to choose the Desktop as the save location for the workbook after selecting This PC.
caption.expectation: The Desktop location should become selected or open as the active save destination, leading toward the save dialog or showing Desktop as the chosen folder.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The visible entry area is the Windows Save As dialog's "文件名(N):" input box near the bottom. The text field currently contains "12", with the insertion caret at the end, indicating the filename is being edited and the new typing will append after the existing text rather than replace it. The full screen shows Excel behind the dialog, saving to the Desktop folder ("桌面"), with file type set to "Excel 工作簿(*.xlsx)".
caption.think: The user is setting the workbook's filename in the Save As dialog and wants to append digits to the current name fragment "12".
caption.action: Type "123" into the "文件名(N):" field, appending it to the existing "12".
caption.expectation: The filename field should update immediately to include the newly typed characters, showing the expanded filename text and leaving the Save As dialog ready for saving.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the rectangular button labeled "保存(S)". This is a standard dialog button in the bottom area of the Windows Save As window, next to the "取消" button and near the "工具(L)" dropdown, so it is a UI control rather than canvas content or a blank region. In the full-screen view, a Save As dialog is open over Excel, the save location is the Desktop, the "文件名(N):" field contains "123", and the file type is "Excel 工作簿(*.xlsx)".
caption.think: The user has already chosen the Desktop and entered the desired filename, so the next step is to confirm the save operation by pressing the Save button.
caption.expectation: The workbook should be saved immediately with the entered filename to the Desktop, and the Save As dialog should close, returning focus to Excel.
